title: Ryanair Invests £40m in Prestwick Airport Maintenance Expansion

sections.heading: What Happened
sections.content: Ryanair has announced a £40 million investment to expand its maintenance facilities at Prestwick Airport in Scotland. This development aims to enhance the airport’s capabilities, making it Ryanair's largest maintenance base in Europe. The investment is expected to significantly increase the operational capacity at Prestwick, providing additional jobs and supporting the local economy.

sections.heading: Why It Matters
sections.content: The expansion at Prestwick Airport highlights Ryanair's commitment to reinforcing its operational infrastructure in the UK. As a major player in the aviation industry, Ryanair's investment is anticipated to bolster economic growth in the region, creating job opportunities and facilitating increased aviation activity. This move could also position Prestwick as a key site for aircraft maintenance services across Europe.

sourceReferences.citationText: bbc-scotland – Ryanair invests £40m in Prestwick airport maintenance expansion
